description: Medium: Make XSD section lookup and streaming section-aware at `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Modules/XsdMetadataEndpointModule.cs:149`, `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Content/ContentProvider.cs:96`, and `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Content/ContentProvider.cs:252` so unknown sections do not fall back to core files and streaming opens the same validated manifest path that section-specific file listing exposed.
acceptance-criteria: `/metadata/xsd/{section}/{file}.xsd` returns 404 for an unknown section instead of falling back to core XSD files.
`ContentProvider.Files(fileFullName, ".xsd", section)` does not include core XSD files when the requested section does not match any manifest project.

`XsdMetadataEndpointModule.GetXsdMetadataFileContent` no longer calls `contentProvider.LoadXsdContent(fileFullName)` after only section-scoped validation; it passes the requested section, or an already resolved validated file path, into XSD content loading.
`ContentProvider` resolves the XSD stream with the same project selection rules used by `Files(fileFullName, ".xsd", section)`.
Duplicate XSD file names across extension projects return the requested section's content; core XSD files remain available to extension sections.
Legacy prefixed XSD names normalize consistently for both section-specific listing and streaming.
steps-to-verify: Add a frontend endpoint/unit test that requests an existing core XSD file through an unknown section and assert `/metadata/xsd/{section}/{file}.xsd` returns 404.
Add a frontend unit test with two extension projects that both contain the same XSD file name and assert `/metadata/xsd/{section}/{file}.xsd` returns the requested section's file content.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Xsd"`

description: Medium: Treat a declared `discoverySpecPath` whose file is missing as an invalid ApiSchema workspace instead of silently skipping it and serving another project's discovery spec.
acceptance-criteria: When a manifest project declares `discoverySpecPath`, a missing file causes `LoadJsonContent("discovery", ...)` to throw an `InvalidOperationException` that identifies the invalid project or path.
Only an omitted `discoverySpecPath` is treated as absent optional content; a declared but missing path does not fall back to a later project.
Existing successful discovery-spec placeholder replacement behavior is unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Add a `ContentProvider` unit test where the core project declares a missing `discoverySpecPath` and a later extension has an existing discovery spec; assert the provider throws instead of returning the extension spec.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~discovery"`

description: Medium: Treat a declared `xsdDirectory` whose directory is missing as an invalid ApiSchema workspace instead of silently returning no XSD files.
acceptance-criteria: When a manifest project declares `xsdDirectory`, a missing directory causes XSD listing or streaming resolution to throw an `InvalidOperationException` that identifies the invalid project or path.
Only an omitted `xsdDirectory` is treated as absent optional XSD content; a declared but missing directory does not make `/metadata/xsd/{section}` look like a valid empty section.
Existing successful XSD enumeration for present directories remains unchanged, including legacy file-name normalization.
steps-to-verify: Add an `ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider` or `ContentProvider` unit test where a project declares a missing `xsdDirectory` and assert XSD enumeration fails with a workspace validation error.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Xsd|FullyQualifiedName~ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider"`

description: Low: Reject nested XSD files consistently in `eng/docker-compose/prepare-dms-schema.ps1:454` and `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Content/ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider.cs:325` so staged or bundled XSD content is not silently omitted by runtime top-level enumeration.
acceptance-criteria: `prepare-dms-schema.ps1` fails before writing the staged ApiSchema manifest when a source project's `xsd` or `XSD` directory contains `.xsd` files below a nested subdirectory.

`ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider.EnumerateValidatedXsdFiles` detects nested `.xsd` files under a declared `xsdDirectory` and throws `InvalidOperationException` instead of returning only top-level files.
Valid top-level `.xsd` files continue to be listed and streamed with the existing file-name normalization behavior.
The error message identifies the project or nested XSD path that must be flattened.
steps-to-verify: Add Pester coverage for `prepare-dms-schema.ps1` with a project containing `xsd/nested/*.xsd` and assert staging fails before producing a successful manifest.
Add frontend unit coverage for `ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider.EnumerateValidatedXsdFiles` with a nested `.xsd` under a declared `xsdDirectory` and assert it throws.
Run: `pwsh -NoProfile -Command "Invoke-Pester -Path ./eng/docker-compose/tests/BootstrapSchemaDeploymentSafety.Tests.ps1"`

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Xsd|FullyQualifiedName~ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider"`

description: Medium: Make ApiSchema manifest path validation symlink-aware in `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Content/ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider.cs:272` so schema-adjacent content cannot escape the configured workspace through linked files or directories.
acceptance-criteria: Schema-adjacent content paths are checked against the canonical workspace root before file opens or XSD enumeration.
A symlinked manifest path or `xsdDirectory` that resolves outside the workspace is rejected with `InvalidOperationException`.
Normal in-workspace relative paths still resolve and stream successfully, and symlink tests are skipped on platforms where symlink creation is unavailable.
steps-to-verify: Add unit coverage for an escaping symlink used as `discoverySpecPath` or `xsdDirectory` and assert the provider rejects it before opening or enumerating the target.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider|FullyQualifiedName~ContentProvider"`

description: Medium: Remove runtime `package-manifest.json` synthesis from `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Content/ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider.cs:87` and make both bundled ApiSchema content and the `SCHEMA_PACKAGES` startup path at `src/dms/run.sh:37` provide the same root `bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` used by bootstrap workspaces.
acceptance-criteria: `ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider.GetManifest` reads only the root `bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` from the configured or bundled ApiSchema workspace and no longer recursively scans for `package-manifest.json` files.
The build or publish path materializes `ApiSchema/bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` for bundled ApiSchema package content, with project paths relative to the bundled `ApiSchema` directory.

When `AppSettings__UseApiSchemaPath=true` and `SCHEMA_PACKAGES` contains package entries, `src/dms/run.sh` downloads the current packages and materializes `AppSettings__ApiSchemaPath/bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` before launching DMS.
The startup-materialized manifest includes only projects from the current `SCHEMA_PACKAGES` list and uses paths relative to `AppSettings__ApiSchemaPath`.
Running with `UseApiSchemaPath=false` loads bundled schema, discovery spec, and XSD content through the same manifest path validation used when `UseApiSchemaPath=true`.

A stray nested `package-manifest.json` under the ApiSchema workspace is ignored; runtime content loading is driven only by projects and paths declared in the root `bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json`.
steps-to-verify: Add or update frontend unit coverage for `ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider` showing that a missing root `bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` fails even when a nested `package-manifest.json` exists.
Add build or publish verification showing `ApiSchema/bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` is produced for bundled package content.

Add startup or script-level coverage showing `src/dms/run.sh` materializes `AppSettings__ApiSchemaPath/bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` from the current `SCHEMA_PACKAGES` entries after download and before launching DMS.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider|FullyQualifiedName~ContentProvider"`

description: Medium: Validate `packageId` as a single safe path segment in `src/dms/clis/EdFi.DataManagementService.ApiSchemaDownloader/Services/ApiSchemaDownloader.cs:53` and `src/dms/clis/EdFi.DataManagementService.ApiSchemaDownloader/Services/ApiSchemaDownloader.cs:201` before using it to construct extraction directories or package filenames.
acceptance-criteria: `ExtractApiSchemaFiles` rejects null, empty, rooted, separator-containing, current-directory, and parent-directory `packageId` values before calling `Path.Combine(outputDir, "Packages", packageId)`.
`DownloadNuGetPackageAsync` applies the same `packageId` validation before constructing the downloaded `.nupkg` file path.
Invalid `packageId` values fail with an `InvalidOperationException` or argument validation exception that identifies `packageId` as invalid.
Valid NuGet package IDs such as `EdFi.DataStandard52.ApiSchema` continue to download and extract into `ApiSchema/Packages/<packageId>`.
steps-to-verify: Add `ApiSchemaDownloader` tests for `packageId` values containing `/`, `\`, `..`, `.`, rooted paths, and whitespace, and assert extraction rejects them before creating directories outside the configured output directory.
Add `ApiSchemaDownloader` coverage showing `DownloadNuGetPackageAsync` rejects an invalid `packageId` before creating the `.nupkg` path.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/clis/EdFi.DataManagementService.ApiSchemaDownloader.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.ApiSchemaDownloader.Tests.Integration.csproj`

description: Medium: Enforce bootstrap workspace-relative path validation in `eng/docker-compose/load-dms-seed-data.ps1:1250` and `eng/docker-compose/load-dms-seed-data.ps1:1332` before reading the staged ApiSchema manifest or copying staged XSDs, so seed loading cannot read XSD content from outside `.bootstrap/ApiSchema`.
acceptance-criteria: `Copy-XsdsFromStagedApiSchema` resolves `schema.apiSchemaManifestPath` through `Resolve-BootstrapWorkspaceRelativePath`, or equivalent validation, before calling `Get-Content`.

Each project `xsdDirectory` is treated as manifest-relative to the staged ApiSchema workspace and rejects rooted paths, empty path segments, current-directory segments, parent-directory segments, and resolved paths outside that workspace before `Test-Path`, `Get-ChildItem`, or `Copy-Item` runs.
Invalid `apiSchemaManifestPath` or `xsdDirectory` values fail with an error that identifies the offending field or project, and no XSD files are copied from an escaping path.
Valid staged `xsdDirectory` values still copy XSD files into the seed workspace `xsd` directory.
steps-to-verify: Add Pester coverage in `eng/docker-compose/tests/BootstrapSeedDelivery.Tests.ps1` for absolute and parent-traversing `xsdDirectory` values in the staged ApiSchema manifest and assert `Copy-XsdsFromStagedApiSchema` throws before copying files.

Add Pester coverage that exercises `Copy-XsdsFromStagedApiSchema` with an invalid `schema.apiSchemaManifestPath` value and assert the helper rejects it through the workspace-relative path validation path.
Run: `pwsh -NoProfile -Command "Invoke-Pester -Path ./eng/docker-compose/tests/BootstrapSeedDelivery.Tests.ps1"`

description: Medium: Clear generated ApiSchema package extraction directories before materializing the package-backed workspace so packages removed from `SCHEMA_PACKAGES` are omitted from the generated root `bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` after a container restart.
acceptance-criteria: The startup package-download path removes stale generated package extraction output under `AppSettings__ApiSchemaPath` before invoking the downloader or manifest materializer for the current `SCHEMA_PACKAGES` list.
Cleanup runs only when `SCHEMA_PACKAGES` contains package entries, so bootstrap runs with an empty package list do not attempt to write into the read-only mounted ApiSchema workspace.
Workspace files that are not generated package extraction output, such as `JsonSchemaForApiSchema.json`, are preserved.
The generated root `bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` contains only projects from the current `SCHEMA_PACKAGES` list.
steps-to-verify: Add script-level or integration coverage that starts with stale generated package extraction output for `<RemovedPackage>`, processes a package list that omits it, and verifies the generated root `bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` excludes `<RemovedPackage>`.
Run the focused startup, downloader, or manifest-materializer verification added for this behavior.

description: Medium: Skip default Debezium connector setup for full Docker-stack bootstrap starts, not only DMS-only starts.
acceptance-criteria: `eng/docker-compose/start-local-dms.ps1` skips `setup-connectors.ps1` when bootstrap mode is active in the full-stack startup path.
`eng/docker-compose/start-published-dms.ps1` skips `setup-connectors.ps1` when bootstrap mode is active in the full-stack startup path.
Non-bootstrap full-stack starts still run connector setup unless `-SkipConnectorSetup` is supplied.
The existing DMS-only bootstrap connector skip behavior remains unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Add or update Pester/static coverage that asserts the full-stack connector setup branch in both startup scripts is guarded by bootstrap mode.
Run the focused bootstrap startup script tests for `eng/docker-compose`.

description: High: Include `src/dms/Directory.Build.targets` in the Docker build context at `src/dms/Dockerfile:12` and copy published `ApiSchema/**` content recursively at `src/dms/Dockerfile:66` so bundled ApiSchema content, including the generated root manifest, survives into the final image.
acceptance-criteria: `src/dms/Dockerfile` copies `Directory.Build.targets` into `/source` before restore/publish so the package-content publish target participates in Docker builds.

The final `src/dms/Dockerfile` image includes the published `ApiSchema/**` content recursively, including the build/publish-generated `ApiSchema/bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json`, discovery/spec files, and XSD files.
Top-level bundled ApiSchema JSON files that are currently copied remain available in the image.
`src/dms/Dockerfile` preserves the bundled root manifest produced by build/publish and does not generate or synthesize ApiSchema manifests itself.
steps-to-verify: Add a Dockerfile or image-content verification that confirms recursive `ApiSchema` content is copied into the final image.
Build or inspect the DMS image output and verify `/app/ApiSchema/bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json`, discovery/spec files, and XSD files are present.

description: Medium: Make `ApiSchemaProvider` load runtime schema JSON from the selected workspace contract instead of recursively loading every `ApiSchema*.json` under `ApiSchemaPath`, so stale legacy root files or stray package outputs cannot change the API surface independently from `bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json`.
acceptance-criteria: When a root `bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` exists, `ApiSchemaProvider` loads exactly the manifest-declared `schemaPath` entries, or an explicitly documented normalized schema convention, and ignores `ApiSchema*.json` files outside that selected set.

A workspace containing a current package-backed manifest plus legacy root-level `ApiSchema*.json` files produced by the old DLL-resource downloader starts with only the manifest-selected core and extensions.

A missing manifest-declared schema file fails fast with a configuration or filesystem error that identifies the missing path; it does not silently fall back to another `ApiSchema*.json` file in the workspace.
Bundled `UseApiSchemaPath=false` loading and `UseApiSchemaPath=true` loading use the same selected-schema behavior when the root manifest is present.
Existing supported legacy loading behavior without a root manifest is either preserved with focused coverage or intentionally removed with an explicit migration note.
steps-to-verify: Add `ApiSchemaProvider` unit coverage with a package-backed-style workspace containing `bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json`, `Packages/<Current>/ApiSchema.json`, and a stale root-level `ApiSchema.json`; assert only the manifest-selected schema is loaded.
Add `ApiSchemaProvider` unit coverage where `bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` declares a missing `schemaPath`; assert startup fails instead of loading an undeclared fallback schema.
Add bundled-output coverage, or equivalent static verification, showing the application `ApiSchema/bootstrap-api-schema-manifest.json` drives bundled schema loading when present.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ApiSchemaProvider"`

description: High: Preserve deterministic core-first XSD metadata listing order in file-mode ApiSchema workspaces so `/metadata/xsd/{section}/files` matches the existing exact response contract.
acceptance-criteria: `ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider.EnumerateValidatedXsdFiles` does not depend on filesystem enumeration order; files from each declared `xsdDirectory` are returned in a deterministic bare-file-name order after validation.
Core section file listings return core XSD names in the same deterministic order expected by `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/General/XSDMetadata.feature`.
Extension section file listings return core XSD names first, followed by that extension's XSD names, with deterministic ordering inside each group.
Section-specific XSD streaming still opens the requested section's validated file path and does not regress duplicate extension filename behavior.
Frontend unit tests assert ordered file lists rather than only asserting containment.
steps-to-verify: Add or update `ContentProvider` unit tests for core and extension section listings to assert exact file order, including core-first ordering for extension sections.
Add or update `XsdMetadataEndpointModule` file-mode tests to assert ordered `/metadata/xsd/{section}/files` response bodies.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Xsd|FullyQualifiedName~ContentProvider|FullyQualifiedName~ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider"`

description: Medium: Validate ApiSchema manifest project fields and non-empty content path values before use so malformed manifests cannot null-reference or treat the workspace root as an XSD directory.
acceptance-criteria: `ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider.GetManifest` rejects missing, null, or blank required project fields such as `projectName`, `projectEndpointName`, and `schemaPath` with an `InvalidOperationException` that identifies the field.
Optional `discoverySpecPath` and `xsdDirectory` remain nullable, but when present they must be non-blank manifest-relative paths that pass the existing rooted-path and parent-traversal validation.
A manifest entry with `xsdDirectory` set to an empty string does not resolve to the ApiSchema workspace root or enumerate unrelated project XSD files.
`ContentProvider.FindProjectForSection` and related content paths no longer rely on non-null record fields that were never validated after JSON deserialization.
Existing valid manifests with omitted optional discovery spec or XSD directory fields continue to work.
steps-to-verify: Add `ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider` unit tests for missing, null, and blank `projectName`, `projectEndpointName`, and `schemaPath` values.
Add unit coverage for blank `discoverySpecPath` and blank `xsdDirectory`; assert both fail with manifest validation errors while omitted optional fields still behave as absent optional content.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ApiSchemaAssetManifestProvider|FullyQualifiedName~ContentProvider"`

description: Medium: Verify user-staged claimset fragments during Story 04 bootstrap startup instead of continuing to mark them `stagedOnly` and deferring them as if CMS still ignored the staged claims workspace.
acceptance-criteria: `eng/docker-compose/prepare-dms-claims.ps1` no longer marks `-ClaimsDirectoryPath` fragment checks as `stagedOnly` solely because they came from the staged workspace.

`eng/docker-compose/bootstrap-claims-gate.psm1` verifies user-staged leaf checks against `/authorizationMetadata` after Story 04 activation; only parent-claim checks that are not directly observable remain deferred.
Bootstrap claim warnings and comments no longer state that CMS does not load staged claims until DMS-1154.
If a user-staged leaf claim is missing from CMS authorization metadata, the claims-ready gate fails the bootstrap instead of passing with only the baseline probe verified.
Existing parent-resource-claim deferral behavior remains intact for checks marked `isParent`.
steps-to-verify: Update `eng/docker-compose/tests/BootstrapSchemaAndSecuritySelection.Tests.ps1` so user-supplied fragment checks are not expected to carry `stagedOnly` after Story 04 activation.
Update `eng/docker-compose/tests/BootstrapClaimsGate.Tests.ps1` so a missing user-staged leaf check fails, while an observed user-staged leaf check passes and parent checks still defer.
Run: `pwsh -NoProfile -Command "Invoke-Pester -Path ./eng/docker-compose/tests/BootstrapSchemaAndSecuritySelection.Tests.ps1,./eng/docker-compose/tests/BootstrapClaimsGate.Tests.ps1"`

description: Medium: Include `published-config.yml` for published bootstrap starts whenever `bootstrapMode` is active so staged CMS claims are mounted with the staged DMS ApiSchema workspace in direct start-script workflows.
acceptance-criteria: `eng/docker-compose/start-published-dms.ps1` includes `published-config.yml` when `$bootstrapMode` is true, including keycloak-backed direct full-stack starts without `-EnableConfig` or `-InfraOnly`.
The published bootstrap compose file set activates the staged claims mount through `DMS_CONFIG_CLAIMS_MOUNT_SOURCE` at the same boundary where `bootstrap-dms.yml` mounts `/app/ApiSchema` for DMS.
Non-bootstrap published keycloak starts continue to honor the existing `-EnableConfig` behavior.
Published wrapper flows that already force configuration service startup continue to work without relying on wrapper-only behavior for the direct start-script contract.
Static or Pester coverage asserts the published and local bootstrap start scripts both include their config compose files for bootstrap mode.
steps-to-verify: Add or update Pester coverage around `start-published-dms.ps1` compose-file selection to assert `published-config.yml` is included when a bootstrap manifest makes `$bootstrapMode` true.

Add a negative Pester assertion showing a non-bootstrap keycloak published start without `-EnableConfig` still does not include `published-config.yml` unless that remains the intended non-bootstrap contract.
Run: `pwsh -NoProfile -Command "Invoke-Pester -Path ./eng/docker-compose/tests/BootstrapEntryPointWorkflow.Tests.ps1,./eng/docker-compose/tests/BootstrapSchemaAndSecuritySelection.Tests.ps1"`

description: Low: Correct the sibling Story 05 and Story 06 ApiSchema package ID examples so extension packages consistently use the canonical `EdFi.DataStandard52.<Project>.ApiSchema` convention.
acceptance-criteria: `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/16-bootstrap/05-metaed-apischema-asset-packaging.md` says extension packages follow `EdFi.DataStandard52.<Project>.ApiSchema`.
`reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/16-bootstrap/06-package-backed-standard-schema-selection.md` says extension packages follow `EdFi.DataStandard52.<Project>.ApiSchema`.

The sibling story wording matches `reference/design/backend-redesign/design-docs/bootstrap/apischema-container.md` and the package IDs used by `src/Directory.Packages.props`, `src/dms/Directory.Build.targets`, and `SCHEMA_PACKAGES` examples.

No remaining bootstrap design or story document refers to the stale `EdFi.DataStandard52.ApiSchema.<Project>` extension package convention except when explicitly describing legacy or rejected examples.
steps-to-verify: Search the bootstrap design/story docs for `EdFi.DataStandard52.ApiSchema.<Project>` and confirm only intentional legacy references remain.

Search `src`, `eng`, and `reference/design/backend-redesign` for `EdFi.DataStandard52.Sample.ApiSchema`, `EdFi.DataStandard52.Homograph.ApiSchema`, and `EdFi.DataStandard52.TPDM.ApiSchema` to verify examples use the canonical extension package IDs.
